title: Rate limiter trusts X-Real-IP/X-Forwarded-For without reverse proxy guarantee
description: server.rs handle_inbox() reads client_ip from X-Real-IP or X-Forwarded-For headers, falling back to 'unknown'. These headers are trivially spoofable when the server is accessed directly (without a reverse proxy). The code has a comment warning about this, but there is no configuration to disable header-based IP detection or to use the actual connection peer address. A direct-access deployment (common during development/testing) has no effective rate limiting — every request can spoof a unique IP. Additionally, the 'unknown' fallback means all requests without these headers share a single bucket. Fix: accept a config flag like 'trust_proxy: true' and use the connection peer address when false. Axum's ConnectInfo extractor provides the real peer address.
close_reason: Documented limitation. Code has WARNING comment. Adding socket peer address requires ConnectInfo which needs deployment-specific reverse proxy config.
